comment: Base movement kit — the full verb set as data: ground move + jump (coyote/buffer), slide, crouch-walk, wall-run + wall-jump, vault/mantle, and kill-plane respawn. This is the reference semantics consumed by MovementInterpreter; it reproduces scripts/player_controller.gd exactly (parity gate: tests/movement_behavior_test.gd). See docs/decisions/movement-substrate.md.

params.comment_thresholds: §D — kernel-baked numbers lifted to params at their CURRENT values (so traces stay bit-identical): wall-verticality acceptance and the slope deadzone angle.

comment_vars: §B — declared numeric state vars generalizing timers/cooldowns/counters. decay:true (the default for undeclared names) reduces the var by dt once per tick (countdown timers). decay:false vars are counters/resources whose change is explicit: jump_hold accumulates while held, slide_steer accumulates via guarded add_var. The decay set here is identical to the pre-refactor hardcoded jump_hold/slide_steer exclusion.

pre_tick.comment: Kill-plane respawn: below the kill plane → teleport to spawn, zero velocity + timers. Aborts the rest of the tick.

transitions.comment: Buffered jump from the ground (highest priority). NOT reenter: the jump sets vy>0 and hands to AIR, whose tick (gravity + move_and_slide) runs this same frame and lifts the body off the floor. Re-entering would re-evaluate AIR's transitions against the STALE is_on_floor()==true (move_and_slide has not run yet at transition-eval time), bouncing AIR->GROUND in the same tick; GROUND's tick then clobbers the jump impulse with ground_snap_bias, so the jump silently dies. Mirrors PlayerController._process_ground, which returns after _do_jump() and does NOT re-run air logic that frame.
Vault: moving fast enough toward a climbable ledge (host captures start/end).
Crouch while fast → SLIDE (deliberate slide-while-running). Entry boost guarded so it can't compound; clamped to max_slide_speed.
Crouch while slow → CROUCH-WALK (lowered, slower, fully steerable).
Left the floor: arm coyote, hand off to AIR this same frame.

tick.comment: Accelerate horizontal velocity toward wish-dir * top_speed; friction when no input.
Level the camera (undo any wall-run roll).

transitions.comment: Coyote jump: buffered jump while still within the coyote window.
Wall-jump within the grace window after leaving a wall.

Wall-run entry (issue #2: require INTENT, don't snap people in). Engages only when the player is fast enough, airborne, beside a wall AND actively giving move input — so it feels deliberate, not an involuntary forward shove. The tick then CARRIES the player's existing along-wall momentum (no forced fixed speed) and lets it decay via the gravity ramp.
Vault while approaching a ledge in the air.

Slide-on-landing: land FAST with crouch held → enter SLIDE directly, CARRYING horizontal momentum (do NOT zero it). This is a DEDICATED landing→slide path (priority 15, above the plain-ground landing at 10) so the momentum-carry case is decided BEFORE the plain-landing zero, never reaching it. The do-block mirrors the GROUND→SLIDE entry exactly (guarded entry boost, cap, crouch collider, slide timers) so a running-crouch landing slides identically whether the crouch was held across the landing or pressed once grounded. Guarded by speed_v<=0 like the plain landing (you land descending, not launching).

Landed into the normal ground state: hand off to GROUND this same frame so a buffered jump fires on the landing frame, and HARD-ZERO horizontal velocity on the landing frame (a dead landing — post-landing momentum is 0 instantly, not decayed over ~60 frames by ground friction). After landing the player walks ONLY if holding a direction (GROUND tick's input-driven accelerate_toward from 0 — that's locomotion, not carried momentum); releasing input stays put. The slide-on-landing case is the higher-priority AIR→SLIDE transition above, so this zero never touches a carried slide. Guarded by speed_v<=0 (not rising) so a FRESH jump (vy>0, is_on_floor() still stale-true at tick-top before move_and_slide) does NOT bounce back to GROUND and get clobbered. You land when descending, not when launching.

tick.comment: Gravity, floaty when holding jump near apex (gated by jump_hold timer + rising).

Air control = STEER ONLY, magnitude-preserving (playtest fix). Holding a direction in the air ROTATES the existing horizontal velocity toward wish-dir at air_steer_rate WITHOUT changing its magnitude — the same carve kernel the slide uses. This removes the old additive Quake-style air-strafe that added net speed off-axis (the spurious gain that survived the landing and caused post-landing slide). From zero horizontal velocity the redirect of a ~zero vector stays ~zero, so a straight-up jump + held direction adds ~nothing horizontally. Bullet jump (a verb) is the one deliberate horizontal/aim launch and is unaffected (it runs on the SLIDE/CROUCH transition, not here). air_acceleration / air_speed_cap are retired (no additive accel); air_steer_rate tunes responsiveness.
Level the camera (undo any wall-run roll once off the wall).

transitions.comment: Airborne mid-slide → AIR, stay crouched (don't pop up). NOTE: jump-from-slide is intentionally absent from the base kit — it is owned by the bullet_jump verb patch (priority 95, triggered by the `jump` input). This keeps the base honest: a slide without any verb overlay has no jump-out path, only the airborne/decay/release exits below. The verb adds the one and only jump transition, with no unreachable duplicate at a lower priority.
Exit while crouch STILL HELD → CROUCH-WALK (stay low; velocity preserved). Fires when decayed / timed-out / steered-out.
Exit with crouch RELEASED or decayed/timed-out/steered-out → GROUND, stand up if headroom.

tick.comment: Downhill slope acceleration, then hard-cap to max_slide_speed (prevents compounding).
Carve: steer velocity direction toward wish-dir at slide_steer_accel WITHOUT changing magnitude.

Sustained forward-aligned input accumulates slide_steer (stands you up); else decays. §A+§B: the old add_timer accumulate/else-decay is now TWO guarded add_var effects — accumulate (+dt) WHEN the alignment gate holds, decay (-dt, floored at 0) WHEN it does not. The two guards are mutually exclusive so exactly one runs per tick, numerically identical to the old single gated add_timer. The steered-out exit reads this var.
tick.name: slide_steer

tick.comment: Else-branch decay of slide_steer toward 0 (matches the old add_timer's maxf(0, t - dt)). Runs only WHEN the accumulate gate above is false (the negated alignment condition).
tick.name: slide_steer

transitions.comment: Left the floor while crouching → AIR (arm coyote). NOTE: jump-from-crouch is intentionally absent from the base kit — it is owned by the bullet_jump verb patch (priority 95, triggered by the `jump` input). The base-without-verb has no jump-out path from CROUCH; the verb is the sole provider, with no unreachable duplicate.
Sped up enough → re-enter SLIDE (e.g. arrived from a slide and pushed back up to speed).
Crouch released → stand (if headroom) and return to GROUND, momentum preserved.

tick.comment: Fully steerable at reduced top speed; friction when no input.

transitions.comment: Wall-jump off the wall.
Wall ended (no longer near) → AIR, arm wall-jump grace.
Wall-run timed out → AIR.
No movement input at all → step off the wall.
Landed → GROUND.

tick.comment: Gravity ramps from near-zero to full over the wall-run as a CURVE (data, not code), clamped fall speed. effective = wall_run_gravity_scale + (1-scale)*frac^ramp.
Run along the wall tangent: forward input → wall_run_speed, backward → 0 (decelerate, never forward), lateral ignored, no input → hold momentum.

Wall-run camera tilt (issue #4): roll the camera toward the wall. Magnitude is data (wall_run_camera_tilt); the SIGN is -wall_side (computed deterministically by the interpreter from the tracked wall) via from_wall_side. Render-only (excluded from the trajectory hash). The level states below lerp it back to 0.

transitions.comment: Vault complete (timer drained) → GROUND.

tick.comment: Scripted move from captured start to captured end over the vault timer.
